content.full_text:
This is in response to your request for advice under the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since we do not have a copy of yourproposed flyer to
review, your request is being treated as a request for informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
Flrn Por,rrrclr, PnacucBs ComrurssroN
P.O. Box 80'l . 428 J Street . Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 31,2001
Raymond Nutting
8161 Happy Valley Road
Somerset, CA 95684
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-01-096
Dear Mr. Nutting:
May you use approximately $325 from your existing2}}7 county supervisor
campaign account to conduct exploratory activities into your viability as a candidate for
county assessor?
CONCLUSION
Yes. If the activities are truly "exploratory" as described below, you may make
these expenditures from your existing 2007 county supervisor committee.
However, if the flyers you intend to distribute promote your candidacy or list your
potential qualifications as county assessor, your activities are not "exploratory." you
have become a candidate for assessor and must open a campaign bank account for that
office.
' Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not proviJe the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3), a copy of which is enclosed.)
?E


Advice Letter No. I-01-096
Page 2
FACTS
You served as an El Dorado County Supervisor for two terms between 1993 and
2000. You opened a new committee to run for county supervisor in2O07. However, you
wish to conduct exploratory activities to determine if you would be a viable candidate for
county assessor in2002. You would like to use approximately $325 out of your existing
county supervisor committee to print flyers and pay rent on a booth at the county fair.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that committees disclose, at specified times, all contributions
and expenditures. (section 84200, et seq.) The term "expenditure," is defined, in
pertinent part, as a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party,
or an enforceable promise to make a payment, unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes. (Section 82025.) An expentiture
to seek office is a payment made for "political purposes." The expenditure for the flyers
and rental of the booth at the county fair to conduct exploratory activities is a permissible
expenditure. However, this expenditure can be made from your supervisorial committee
only if the activity is truly exploratory. (Section 85201.)
In your letter, you did not indicate what information will be contained in your
flyers. When a person conducts a survey to determine what issues are considered most
important to voters, such activity would be exploratory as long as the survey does not list
the potential candidate's qualifications, imply that the person is qualified for a particular
office, or list the names of persons who are supporting the individual's candidacy. If an
individual ultimately decides to become a candidate, payments in connection with the
exploratory activity would then be disclosed. (Powell Advice Letter, No. 4-85-241.)
However, if your flyers list, or imply, your qualifications as described above, this is not
exploratory activity. Rather, you are essentially advocating and promoting your election.
This activity would make you a candidate for the office of county *s"soi.i
If this is the case, you must file a candidate intention (Form 501) and open a
campaign bank account in connection with your county assessor election. (Sections
85200 and 85201.) The funds you intend to use for the flyer and rental of the fair booth
' "Candidate" means an individual who ... receives a contribution or makes an expenditure...with a view to
bringing about his or her nomination or election to any elective office, whether or not the specific elective
office for which he or she will seek nomination or election is known at the time the contribution is received
or the expendihre is made.


Advice Letter No. I-01-096
Page 3
must be transferred from your 2007 supervisorial committee into the county assessor
bank account prior to expenditure.
I trust this answers your question. Feel free to call me at (916) 322-5660 if you
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Jeanette E.
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
LM/IETtjt
